clusters.title: The One Nation vote in SA
clusters.neutral_headline: One Nation Achieves Breakthrough In SA Election

items.title: The One Nation vote in SA
items.standfirst: One Nation just sent a shiver through the Liberal Party, after a breakthrough result in South Australia. Pauline Hanson's party achieved more than 21 per cent of the statewide primary vote, beating the Liberal Party and becoming the second-highest polling party. But nationally, more attention has been on the undercard fight, and that has left the Liberal Party deeply wounded.
items.content: One Nation just sent a shiver through the Liberal Party, after a breakthrough result in South Australia. Pauline Hanson's party achieved more than 21 per cent of the statewide primary vote, beating the Liberal Party and becoming the second-highest polling party. But nationally, more attention has been on the undercard fight, and that has left the Liberal Party deeply wounded.

items.title: Why isn't One Nation winning many seats in the SA election despite its high vote?
items.standfirst: One Nation has received more than 20 per cent of the primary vote in South Australia, and so far, its lower house seat count remains slight. Why is that, and why are onlookers so interested in the result?
items.content: One Nation has received more than 20 per cent of the primary vote in South Australia, and so far, its lower house seat count remains slight. Why is that, and why are onlookers so interested in the result?
